text: The beginning of this verse reads literally, ‘Now thou shalt be laid waste, daughter of freebooters’; in the Hebrew text, ‘thou shalt be laid waste’ is ‘form thyself into bands’ (or possibly ‘cut thyself’, i.e. in sign of mourning). Thus both the meaning of the verse and its historical background are obscure; but conceivably the prophet is preaching guerrilla warfare, and reminding his fellow-countrymen that this was, in early days, king David’s experience.

Our present Hebrew text reads, ‘Thou (art) too small to be among the clans of Juda’; but there may have been an alternative reading, ‘thou art not small among the clans of Juda’. This would account for the rendering given in Mt. 2.6, without making it necessary to suppose that the translator of St Matthew’s Aramaic was guilty of an error. ‘From ages untold’; literally, ‘from the days of eternity’, but this is a phrase used somewhat loosely by Hebrew authors (cf. 7.20 below), and no certain theological inferences can be based on it.
Literally, ‘he abandons them’, but the sense is presumably that given.
Apparently the brethren of the Messianic prince; the Septuagint Greek gives ‘their brethren’, but this is probably guesswork.
